text: According to VSEPR theory, the shape of \( \text{NH}_3 \) is:
explanation: \(\text{NH}_3\): 3 bond pairs + 1 lone pair → trigonal pyramidal shape (tetrahedral electron geometry).
name: Shape of NH₃

text: The bond angle in \( \text{H}_2\text{O} \) is approximately:
explanation: \(\text{H}_2\text{O}\): 2 bond pairs + 2 lone pairs. Lone pair repulsion reduces angle from 109.5° to ≈104.5°.
name: Bond Angle of H₂O

text: The hybridisation of carbon in \( \text{BF}_3 \) (Boron, not Carbon) is:
explanation: Boron in \(\text{BF}_3\): 3 bond pairs, 0 lone pairs → \(sp^2\) hybridisation → trigonal planar.
name: Hybridisation in BF₃

text: Which of the following has the highest bond order?
name: Highest Bond Order

explanation: MO Bond Order \( = \frac{N_b - N_a}{2} \), where \(N_b\) = bonding electrons, \(N_a\) = antibonding electrons.
name: MO Bond Order Formula

explanation: \(\text{PCl}_5\): 5 bond pairs, 0 lone pairs, \(sp^3d\) hybridisation → trigonal bipyramidal.
name: Shape of PCl₅

explanation: Higher electronegativity difference → more ionic character. \(\Delta\chi > 1.7\): predominantly ionic; \(< 1.7\): covalent.
name: Electronegativity and Bond Type

text: A sigma (σ) bond is formed by:
options: Overlap of d and p orbitals only
explanation: σ bond: end-to-end (head-on) overlap along internuclear axis. π bond: lateral (sideways) overlap.
name: Sigma Bond Formation

text: The hybridisation of carbon in ethyne (\( \text{C}_2\text{H}_2 \)) is:
explanation: In \(\text{C}_2\text{H}_2\) (acetylene): triple bond between C atoms → \(sp\) hybridisation, linear shape (180°).
name: Hybridisation in Ethyne

text: Which molecule has zero dipole moment despite polar bonds?
explanation: \(\text{CO}_2\) is linear; two C=O dipoles cancel → net dipole = 0. \(\text{H}_2\text{O}\) and \(\text{NH}_3\) have net dipole.
name: Zero Dipole Moment

text: The number of lone pairs on the central atom of \( \text{XeF}_4 \) is:
explanation: Xe in \(\text{XeF}_4\): 4 bond pairs + 2 lone pairs → \(sp^3d^2\) → square planar shape.
name: Lone Pairs in XeF₄

text: Resonance structures of \( \text{O}_3 \) show that the actual O–O bond order is:
explanation: Ozone has two resonance structures (one O=O and one O–O). Average bond order = (1+2)/2 = 1.5.
name: Resonance in Ozone

explanation: H-bonding strength depends on electronegativity and size of atom. F is most electronegative and smallest → strongest H-bond in HF.
name: Strongest Hydrogen Bond

text: In \( \text{SF}_6 \), the hybridisation of S is:
explanation: \(\text{SF}_6\): 6 bond pairs, 0 lone pairs → \(sp^3d^2\) hybridisation → octahedral shape.
name: Hybridisation in SF₆

text: According to MO theory, which molecule is paramagnetic?
explanation: \(\text{O}_2\) MO configuration has 2 unpaired electrons in \(\pi^*\) orbitals → paramagnetic. This was a triumph of MO theory.
name: Paramagnetic Molecule
